title: A Stranger at the Gate
description: You have arrived at Concordia's east gate. The Iron Wardens process arrivals with practiced efficiency. A fellow traveler named Kael seems to be having trouble with the entry inspection.

objectives.description: Present yourself to the gate guard
Speak with the other traveler being held up at the gate
Enter Concordia and reach the market district

breadcrumbs.content: Concordia is governed by four factions who signed something called the Founding Compact. The Iron Wardens control the gates. The confiscation list Orin checked included a category for 'pre-Year 70 historical materials' — which struck you as an odd thing to confiscate.

title: The Faction Question
description: Kael has heard that newcomers to Concordia are expected to affiliate with one of the four factions within their first week — or find a patron who can vouch for independent status. The Scholars' Guild has posted a notice in the market offering 'orientation for interested newcomers.' It seems like a reasonable place to start.

objectives.description: Find the Scholars' Guild outpost in the archive quarter
Speak with the scribe running orientation
Collect the faction overview pamphlet Tollan prepares
Find Yshe Dawnmere, who Tollan mentions as someone who 'knows the city the way it actually works'

breadcrumbs.content: Tollan gave you the official overview but became visibly uncomfortable when you asked about the Great Purge. He said it was 'a closed matter.' Then he corrected himself: 'A concluded investigation.' The distinction felt deliberate.

Yshe described the Shadow Network as 'the faction that doesn't exist, officially, but that everyone deals with eventually.' She offered to introduce you to a contact there, 'when you are ready for things to get complicated.'

title: The First Obligation
description: Scribe Tollan has a problem: a crate of recovered texts was confiscated at the east gate this morning under the new 'historical materials' category. The texts are pre-Year 70, legitimate scholarship, and not seditious by any reasonable standard. He needs someone to retrieve them from the gate garrison — but the Wardens will only release confiscated property to a citizen with faction standing. You do not yet have standing. You need to decide whose name to invoke.

objectives.description: Go to the garrison to claim the confiscated texts
Speak with the captain processing the confiscation
Return the recovered texts to Tollan

moral_branch.description: At the garrison, Captain Rael will ask who vouches for you. Your answer determines your initial faction standing.

options.consequence: Rael releases the texts but notes your name as Guild-affiliated. You gain Scholars' Guild standing. Voss will eventually see this report.
Rael releases the texts quickly. You gain Warden standing but Tollan notices the ease of the retrieval and becomes quietly suspicious of you.
Rael respects the honesty. She releases the texts as a personal favor, off the record. You gain no standing but Rael remembers you differently from most people who walk through her garrison.

breadcrumbs.content: Captain Rael told you the confiscation order came from Voss personally. When you asked what was in the texts, she said: 'I did not read them.' It was not a dismissal. It sounded like a decision she had made carefully.
When you returned the texts to Tollan, he examined them quickly and said only: 'These are not the ones he is looking for.' He did not explain who 'he' was.
